title: Empirical rule
topic_path: Module 4: Inferences and conclusions from data

assessment_items.question_content:
The lifespans of lizards in a particular zoo are normally distributed. The average lizard lives $3.1$ years; the standard deviation is $0.6$ years.

 ** Use the empirical rule $(68 - 95 - 99.7\%)$ to estimate the probability of a lizard living longer than $2.5$ years. **  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\%$
hints:
![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 3.1. A vertical line segment rises from 3.1 to the center of the curve. The segment is shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/69043f5a8e7d64f6807fa4ee506daf07b3190243)

  We know the lifespans are normally distributed with an average lifespan of $3.1$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 3.1. A vertical line segment rises from 3.1 to the center of the curve. 2 additional vertical segments, each shaded pink, rise from points on the axis marked as 2.5 and 3.7 to the curve.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/342793d4ed11cb9c19bda6fadb2d7796db67e27d)

  We know the standard deviation is $0.6$ years, so one standard deviation below the mean is $2.5$ years and one standard deviation above the mean is $3.7$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 3.1. A vertical line segment rises from 3.1 to the center of the curve. 4 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 1.9, 2.5, 3.7 and 4.3, to the curve. The segments at 1.9 and 4.3 are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/522eacd7640a4c8878d27c93ad4fe1b87553481a)

  Two standard deviations below the mean is $1.9$ years and two standard deviations above the mean is $4.3$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 3.1. A vertical line segment rises from 3.1 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 1.3, 1.9, 2.5, 3.7, 4.3 and 4.9, to the curve. The segments at 1.3 and 4.9 are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f864dd9717d0962f1d79ba6e3f4fc8b8d7c41626)

  Three standard deviations below the mean is $1.3$ years and three standard deviations above the mean is $4.9$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 3.1. A vertical line segment rises from 3.1 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 1.3, 1.9, 2.5, 3.7, 4.3 and 4.9, to the curve. The segment at 2.5 is shaded pink. An arrow, beneath the axis, points rightward from a solid circle at 2.5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/13ce457ac37527b59975055589a8534a2329f31b)

  We are interested in the probability of a lizard living longer than $2.5$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 3.1. A vertical line segment rises from 3.1 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 1.3, 1.9, 2.5, 3.7, 4.3 and 4.9, to the curve. The segment at 2.5 is shaded pink. An arrow, beneath the axis, points rightward from a solid circle at 2.5. The area beneath the curve, but above the axis, between 2.5 and 3.7, is shaded. An arrow indicates the shaded space is made up of 68% of the data.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7a106d41f3b82e13c3539073f9bbbf06445cb7c2)

  The empirical rule (or the $68-95-99.7\%$ rule) tells us that $\green{68\%}$ of the lizards will have lifespans within 1 standard deviation of the average lifespan.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 3.1. A vertical line segment rises from 3.1 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 1.3, 1.9, 2.5, 3.7, 4.3 and 4.9, to the curve. The segment at 2.5 is shaded pink. An arrow, beneath the axis, points rightward from a solid circle at 2.5. The area beneath the curve, but above the axis, between 2.5 and 3.7, is shaded. An arrow indicates the shaded space is made up of 68% of the data. Additional arrows indicate that 16% of the data falls leftward of 2.5 and 16% of the data falls rightward of 3.7.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f8bba1ecf81dce8cb73e7cb8ae303f5554cab9e5)

  The remaining $32\%$ of the lizards will have lifespans that fall outside the shaded area. Because the normal distribution is symmetrical, half $(\green{16\%})$ will live less than $2.5$ years and the other half $(\green{16\%})$ will live longer than $3.7$ years.
The probability of a particular lizard living longer than $2.5$ years is $\green{68\%} + \green{16\%}$, or $84\%$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The lifespans of meerkats in a particular zoo are normally distributed. The average meerkat lives $10.4$ years; the standard deviation is $1.9$ years.

** Use the empirical rule $(68-95-99.7\%)$ to estimate the probability of a meerkat living between $12.3$ and $14.2$ years. **  


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\%$
hints:

  We know the lifespans are normally distributed with an average lifespan of $10.4$ years.

A vertical line segment rises from 10.4 to the center of the curve. 2 additional vertical segments, each shaded pink, rise from points on the axis marked as 8.5 and 12.3 to the curve.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ec6f15f2761a0221792bcb4089eef630a056330)

  We know the standard deviation is $1.9$ years, so one standard deviation below the mean is $8.5$ years and one standard deviation above the mean is $12.3$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 10.4. A vertical line segment rises from 10.4 to the center of the curve. 4 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 6.6, 8.5, 12.3 and 14.2, to the curve. The segments at 6.6 and 14.2 are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4a6d57a1b4c9733d77ed0c2b4b085d5995c77fab)

  Two standard deviations below the mean is $6.6$ years and two standard deviations above the mean is $14.2$ years.

A vertical line segment rises from 10.4 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 4.7, 6.6, 8.5, 12.3, 14.2, and 16.1, to the curve. The segments at 4.7 and 16.1 are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/922cdd6cd999690ada341e2426b5ea1cbd59cfa7)

  Three standard deviations below the mean is $4.7$ years and three standard deviations above the mean is $16.1$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 10.4. A vertical line segment rises from 10.4 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 4.7, 6.6, 8.5, 12.3, 14.2, and 16.1, to the curve. The segments at 12.3 and 14.2 are shaded pink. A horizontal line segment, beneath the axis, begins at a solid circle at 12.3 and ends at a solid circle at 14.2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/993c3697add916cfa8be550b2b9f73689c1e9670)

  We are interested in the probability of a meerkat living between $12.3$ and $14.2$ years.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 10.4. A vertical line segment rises from 10.4 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 4.7, 6.6, 8.5, 12.3, 14.2, and 16.1, to the curve. The segments at 12.3 and 14.2 are shaded pink. A horizontal line segment, beneath the axis, begins at a solid circle at 12.3 and ends at a solid circle at 14.2. The area beneath the curve, but above the axis, between 6.6 and 14.2, is shaded. An arrow indicates the shaded space is made up of 95% of the data.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/83c7cde70ef0b98adb14e6e2198d50952d92cb03)

  The empirical rule (or the $68-95-99.7\%$ rule) tells us that $\green{95\%}$ of the meerkats will have lifespans within $2$ standard deviations of the average lifespan.

![A normal distribution curve is plotted along a horizontal axis. The axis is marked at 10.4. A vertical line segment rises from 10.4 to the center of the curve. 6 additional vertical segments rise from points on the axis marked as 4.7, 6.6, 8.5, 12.3, 14.2, and 16.1, to the curve. The segments at 12.3 and 14.2 are shaded pink. A horizontal line segment, beneath the axis, begins at a solid circle at 12.3 and ends at a solid circle at 14.2. The area beneath the curve, but above the axis, between 6.6 and 14.2, is shaded. An arrow indicates the shaded space is made up of 95% of the data. Additional arrows indicate that 68% of the data falls between 8.5 and 12.3, that 13.5% of the data falls between 6.6 and 8.5, and that 13.5% of the data falls between of 12.3 and 14.2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8fefeab9db1c3b3349d1ecdff6a4799be0936b33)

  It also tells us that $\green{68\%}$ of the meerkats will have lifespans within $1$ standard deviation of the mean. That leaves $95\% - 68\% = 27\%$ of meerkats between $1$ and $2$ standard deviations of the mean, or $\red{13.5\%}$ on either side of the distribution.
The probability of a particular meerkat living between $12.3$ and $14.2$ years is $\red{13.5\%}$.
